issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing `aria-controls` when drawer has no `id`
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the trigger button receives `aria-controls` only if `_drawer.id` exists. If the drawer element lacks an `id` (the default markup does not set one), the button has no `aria-controls`, so screen‑reader users cannot discover the controlled region.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing `aria-controls` when panel lacks `id`
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.register()` the trigger receives `aria-controls` only if the panel already has an `id`. If the panel is missing an `id` (common in the default markup), the trigger has no `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing `aria-controls` when FAQ item has no `id`
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqRegistry.parse()` the trigger’s `aria-controls` is set to the panel’s `id`. If the FAQ item (the container) does not have a `data-faq-id` or an explicit `id`, the panel may lack an `id`, leaving the trigger without `aria-controls`. This disconnect prevents screen readers from linking the button to its answer region.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not have an initial `aria-pressed` state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: In `pricing._syncToggleState()` the buttons receive `aria-pressed` only after a period change occurs. On initial load the buttons lack `aria-pressed`, so assistive technologies cannot tell which period is active.

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter chips update UI without an `aria-live` announcement (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: `activeFilterChips.render()` rebuilds the chip list but never announces the change to a live region. Users of screen readers are unaware when filters are added or removed via the chips.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form validation does not set `aria-invalid` on the email field when empty/invalid (JS‑D)
issuesFound.explanation: `newsletterForm._onSubmit()` validates the email with a regex but never marks the `<input type="email">` as invalid via `aria-invalid`. Users of assistive technologies receive no indication of the error.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search status element is not announced with `aria-live="assertive"` for error messages (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateStatus()` writes error text to `_statusEl`, but the element is only marked with `aria-live="polite"`. Errors (e.g., “Search failed”) should be announced assertively to ensure immediate awareness.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slide changes are announced, but the “previous” and “next” buttons lack `aria-controls` pointing to the carousel list (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation buttons (`data-carousel-prev` / `data-carousel-next`) control the slide list but do not have `aria-controls` attributes, so screen‑reader users cannot discover the relationship.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard “Previous” button does not have `aria-controls` or `aria-label` describing the step it returns to (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard’s navigation buttons (`data-wizard-prev`, `data-wizard-next`) change the visible step but lack `aria-controls` or a descriptive `aria-label`. Assistive technologies cannot convey which step will be shown.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form validates the e‑mail field with a custom regex, but when the value is invalid it only shows a status message and does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the `<input type="email">`. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the error.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer lacks aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close()` function hides the drawer by removing CSS classes and unlocking scroll, but it never adds `aria-hidden="true"` to the drawer element. Screen readers may still read the hidden navigation markup.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger may miss aria‑controls linking to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the trigger receives `aria-controls` only if the drawer element already has an `id`. The drawer (`_drawer`) is selected from the DOM but never guaranteed to have an `id`, so many instances end up without `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer always has an `id` (e.g., `if (!_drawer.id) _drawer.id = 'mobile-nav-drawer';`) before setting `aria-controls`, or always set `aria-controls` to the drawer’s id after guaranteeing it exists.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger may miss aria‑controls when panel lacks id
issuesFound.explanation: `dropdownMenu.register()` adds `aria-controls` to the trigger only if the panel element already has an `id`. If a developer forgets to give the panel an `id`, the trigger ends up without `aria-controls`, leaving the disclosure relationship undefined for AT.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` emit custom events but never write to an `aria‑live` region. Users of screen readers receive no audible cue that a submenu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing the panel’s visibility, update a dedicated live region (e.g., `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` or `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')`). Create a hidden `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">` in the page if one does not exist and use it for the announcements.  

---

issuesFound.title: Discount‑code input status messages are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `discountCodeInput._onApply()` writes messages to `_statusEl` but the element lacks `aria-live`, so screen‑reader users are not informed whether the discount code was accepted or rejected.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'assertive')` (or `polite` as appropriate) when creating `_statusEl`, or ensure the existing element has that attribute. Then update its text as before; the live region will announce the change.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are hidden with CSS class only, without aria‑hidden when the hide is performed via class removal (off‑screen technique)
issuesFound.explanation: The hide function `_closeInstance()` removes the `open` class and later adds the `hidden` attribute, but if a developer modifies the hide logic to use an off‑screen technique (e.g., `transform: translateX(-100%)`) without also setting `aria-hidden="true"`, the element remains in the accessibility tree. This pattern is present in the code base for other components (e.g., `mobileNav` drawer) and should be guarded against.
issuesFound.fix:
Whenever a hide operation uses an off‑screen technique instead of `hidden`, explicitly set `aria-hidden="true"` on the element, and remove that attribute when showing it.  

---

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle buttons lack explicit `aria-pressed` state on initial render
issuesFound.explanation: The theme‑switcher UI uses buttons with `data-theme-toggle` attributes, but the initial HTML does not set `aria-pressed`. The script adds `aria-pressed` on click, but AT users cannot know the current state before interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
In the HTML markup, set `aria-pressed="false"` on the default theme button (e.g., `<button data-theme-toggle="light" aria-pressed="false">Light</button>`). The script already updates the attribute on toggle.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels are hidden with `hidden` attribute but lack `aria-hidden` for older browsers that may ignore `hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ `close()` function sets `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` but does not also set `aria-hidden="true"`. Some older assistive technologies rely on `aria-hidden` to determine visibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` when hiding and remove it when showing.  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay (`_overlay`) is hidden with `hidden` attribute but does not have `aria-hidden`, potentially exposing it to AT when off‑screen.
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay element is toggled via `hidden` attribute only. For maximum compatibility, it should also receive `aria-hidden="true"` when hidden and have the attribute removed when shown.
issuesFound.fix: In `_open()` and `_close()`, add `setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'false')` when showing and `setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` when hiding.

issuesFound.title: Carousel play/pause button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The “play/pause” button in the testimonials carousel toggles auto‑play but never updates aria‑pressed, so screen‑reader users cannot tell its state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isPlaying)); and ensure the attribute is present on initial render (false).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav hamburger button toggles a drawer that has no id, so the script never adds aria‑controls to the trigger. Assistive tech cannot associate the button with the navigation panel.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer element has an id (e.g., id="mobile-nav-drawer") or set aria‑controls manually on the trigger.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile‑nav drawer is closed, the code only removes CSS classes; it never sets aria‑hidden="true". The drawer remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: Pricing savings message not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The element showing the “Save X%” badge updates its text but has no aria‑live or role="status", so changes are not spoken.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_savingsMsg, 'role', 'status'); setAttr(_savingsMsg, 'aria-live', 'polite'); or update an existing live region with the message.

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan price updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, price numbers are updated in the DOM but no live‑region is used, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new price.
issuesFound.fix: After updating priceDisplay.textContent, also set priceDisplay.setAttribute('aria-live','polite'); or write the formatted price to a dedicated live region.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing‑period toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The “annual/monthly” switch toggles a CSS class but never updates aria‑pressed, so its binary state is not conveyed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In _onToggleClick add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear)); and initialise the attribute on load.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher buttons may lack initial aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Theme toggle buttons receive aria‑pressed only after themeSwitcher.init runs; before that they have no state, causing a brief period of missing information.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria‑pressed="false" to the markup for each [data‑theme‑toggle] button as a default.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload remove button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The “✕” button that removes a file entry has no aria‑label or aria‑description, making its purpose unclear to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(removeBtn, 'aria-label', `Remove ${entry.file.name}`); when creating the button.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu panel keydown handler does not announce state
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened/closed via keyboard, no live‑region announcement is made, so users are not informed of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After _setExpanded(trigger, true/false) call liveRegion.announce(`Menu ${open ? 'opened' : 'closed'}`); or use a dedicated status region.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list lacks aria‑live
issuesFound.explanation: The autocomplete <ul role="listbox"> is updated with suggestions but has no aria‑live attribute, so changes are not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-live', 'polite'); when mounting the listbox, or update a separate live region with the suggestion count.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” function does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the hamburger trigger when the drawer is shown.
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function adds CSS classes and locks scroll but never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` state, so screen‑reader users cannot know the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of `open()` (or after the drawer becomes visible).

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu click handler opens the panel without updating `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button.
issuesFound.explanation: `_onTriggerClick` toggles the dropdown via `_openInstance` / `_closeInstance`, but `_openInstance` never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger, leaving AT unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_openInstance`, after opening, call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu keyboard handler opens the panel without updating `aria‑expanded` on the trigger.
issuesFound.explanation: In `_onTriggerKeydown`, when handling Enter/Space/ArrowDown/Up, the code opens the dropdown via `_openInstance` but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger.
issuesFound.fix:
After `_openInstance(inst);` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” routine does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the question button when a panel is expanded.
issuesFound.explanation: `open(item, …)` adds CSS classes and animates the panel but never updates the trigger’s `aria-expanded`, so assistive tech cannot detect the opened state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when the item is opened.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion buttons lack an initial `aria‑expanded="false"` attribute on page load.
issuesFound.explanation: When the FAQ component is initialized, triggers are given `aria-controls` but no explicit `aria-expanded` value, leaving the state undefined for AT until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
In the FAQ registry parsing, after creating the trigger, set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` for items that start closed.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced to screen‑reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown opens and closes visually, but no `aria-live` region is updated, so users are unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` (or use a dedicated status element with `aria-live="polite"`).

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden with CSS classes but never receives `aria‑hidden="true"` when closed.
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer’s visibility is controlled by adding/removing CSS classes (`is-open`) without toggling `aria-hidden`, so the element remains in the accessibility tree when hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close()`, after removing the open class, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. In `open()`, remove that attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set `aria‑invalid` on fields that fail validation.
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` uses `srUtils.markInvalid`, which only adds `aria-describedby` but never sets `aria-invalid="true"`. AT therefore cannot detect invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix:
In `markInvalid`, add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`. Likewise, `markValid` should remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle buttons are not given an initial `aria‑pressed` state on page load.
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons (`data-view-mode`) are updated with `aria-pressed` only when a user clicks them; the initially active button lacks the attribute, so its pressed state is ambiguous to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
After determining the initial view mode, set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === currentMode ? 'true' : 'false');` for each button during initialization.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close routine sets `aria-expanded="false"` but does not also set `aria-hidden="true"` on the associated panel, leaving the hidden panel still exposed to AT.
issuesFound.explanation: `close(item, …)` hides the panel with `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the panel element, which is required for some AT to recognize the hidden state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` when the panel is closed, and remove it when opened.

  …

  …

  …
  setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');   // ← add this line
  …

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden without setting `aria‑hidden="true"`
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close()` removes the “open” class from the drawer but never marks the drawer as `aria‑hidden`. The element remains in the accessibility tree while visually hidden, which can cause confusion for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
function close(announceToSR) {
  …
  setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');   // ← add this line
  …
}
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger may lack `aria‑controls` because the drawer has no `id` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the code only adds `aria-controls` when `_drawer.id` exists. If the drawer element lacks an `id` (common in the markup), the trigger ends up without a relationship to its controlled region, breaking the required ARIA linkage.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger may lack `aria‑controls` when the panel has no `id`
issuesFound.explanation: The `_register(trigger, panel)` routine adds `aria-controls` only if the panel already has an `id`. If the panel is missing an `id`, the trigger remains without a required `aria-controls` reference.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter subscription form does not reflect validation errors with `aria‑invalid`
issuesFound.explanation: The email field is validated with `validateField` but never receives `aria‑invalid="true"` when the value is invalid. Users of assistive technology receive no programmatic error indication.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard step changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_onNext()` and `_onPrev()` change the visible step but do not write to any element with `role="status"`/`aria‑live`. Screen‑reader users are unaware that the wizard has moved to a new step.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing steps, add:  
```javascript
liveRegion.announce(
  `Step ${_current + 1} of ${_steps.length} – ${_steps[_current].title}`
);
```

---

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard form submission does not announce success or error via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_onSubmit()` performs the AJAX request and updates the UI, but never announces the result to AT. Users may not know whether the submission succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
In the success branch:  
```javascript
liveRegion.announce('Form submitted successfully');
```  
and in the error branch:  
```javascript
liveRegion.announceAssertive('Form submission failed. Please try again.');
```

---

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard step navigation (next/prev) does not provide a dedicated live‑region announcement (JS‑G)
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard’s visible change is not paired with a dedicated live‑region write; the generic status region used elsewhere is not updated for each step change, violating the “user action with no live‑region announcement” rule.
issuesFound.fix:
As in Issue 8, write to a dedicated live region after each step change.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “open” does not set aria‑expanded to true

issuesFound.title: Removing a filter chip does not announce the change
issuesFound.explanation: activeFilterChips._onChipClick removes a chip but provides no live‑region feedback, so AT users are unaware that a filter was cleared.
issuesFound.fix: After removing the chip, call liveRegion.announce('Filter removed'); or a more specific message.

issuesFound.title: Adding filter chips is not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: activeFilterChips.render() creates chips to represent active filters but does not announce the new filters, leaving AT users uninformed of the current filter state.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering chips, call liveRegion.announce('Filters applied: …'); listing the active filters.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field error message not rendered (broken aria‑describedby)
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for the newsletter email sets aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when there is an error, but no element with that id is ever rendered because <FormGroup> is called without the required errorId and errorMessage props. Screen‑reader users will hear “error” without any associated message.
issuesFound.fix:
Pass the missing props to <FormGroup> so the <ErrorMessage> is output.  

  ```tsx
  // Line 642
  <FormGroup
    label="Email address"
    htmlFor="newsletter-email"
    errorId="newsletter-email-error"
    errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
  >
    <TextInput
      id="newsletter-email"
      type="email"
      name="email"
      placeholder="you@company.com"
      autoComplete="email"
      value={email.state.value}
      onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)}
      onBlur={email.onBlur}
      required
      aria-required="true"
      invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}
      aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}
    />
  </FormGroup>
  ```

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The <button> that toggles the navigation dropdown does not have an aria-expanded attribute reflecting the isOpen state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s visibility.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation main toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger/menu button controlling the mobile navigation panel lacks aria-expanded, so its open/closed state is not conveyed to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Hero video play/pause button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles background video playback uses an aria-label but does not expose its pressed state via aria-pressed, preventing AT users from knowing whether the video is playing.

issuesFound.title: IconButton for carousel previous control missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used as the “previous” control contains only an icon and has no aria-label, making it inaccessible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: IconButton for carousel next control missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used as the “next” control contains only an icon and lacks an aria-label, so it is not announced to AT users.

issuesFound.title: IconButton for mobile navigation close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button inside the mobile navigation panel is an IconButton with only an icon and no aria-label, leaving it unnamed for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 679: <IconButton
Line 680:   aria-label="Close navigation menu"
Line 681:   onClick={onClose}
Line 682:   className="mobile-nav__close"
Line 683: >

issuesFound.title: IconButton for theme toggle missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button renders only an icon (sun/moon/computer) without an aria-label, so its purpose is unclear to screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons indicate selection via visual styling but do not expose their state with aria-pressed, preventing AT users from knowing which filter is active.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual billing buttons act as a toggle but lack aria-pressed, so their selected state is not communicated to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Hero section missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> that contains skip links has no aria-label or aria-labelledby, leaving the landmark unnamed.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field marker not hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required asterisk ( *) is not hidden from AT, causing a stray “*” to be announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1015: * (Note: same fix applies to each required marker in the form.)

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form missing associated label
issuesFound.explanation: The email input is wrapped by FormGroup which renders a <label> without htmlFor, so the input lacks a programmatic label.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1002: <label htmlFor="newsletter-email" className="form-label">

(Adjust the FormGroup component to accept htmlFor and apply it to the label.)

issuesFound.title: Contact form missing associated label
issuesFound.explanation: Each FormGroup in the contact form renders a <label> without htmlFor, leaving the corresponding inputs unlabeled for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Update FormGroup to render <label htmlFor={htmlFor} ...> and pass htmlFor prop from each FormGroup usage (e.g., htmlFor="contact-name").

issuesFound.title: TextInput components missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The custom TextInput component does not forward the invalid prop to an aria-invalid attribute, so when a field is invalid AT users are not notified.
issuesFound.fix: In TextInput component, add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the <input> element.

issuesFound.title: TextInput aria-describedby references non‑existent error IDs
issuesFound.explanation: Each input’s aria-describedby points to an ID (e.g., “newsletter-email-error”) that is never rendered, breaking the error‑message association.
issuesFound.fix: Render an <ErrorMessage> with the matching id for each field or remove the aria-describedby when no error element exists.

issuesFound.title: Submit button in NewsletterForm missing aria-busy for loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The submit <Button> uses a non‑standard “loading” prop, so the loading state is not communicated via aria-busy.
issuesFound.fix: Change the prop to isLoading={isSubmitting} and ensure the Button receives aria-busy automatically.

issuesFound.title: Submit button in ContactForm missing aria-busy for loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form’s submit button also uses “loading” instead of “isLoading”, so its busy state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Change to isLoading={isSubmitting} on the <Button> component.

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The “Products” link is marked as current via className but lacks aria-current="page", so AT cannot identify the current page.
